project.name: Module 7: BIP39 Recovery
project.description: Create lib/recovery.ts — BIP39 mnemonic phrase generation, validation, and recovery key derivation. Users generate a 12-word recovery phrase during setup, write it down, and can use it to recover data if they forget their password. The recovery module stores a 'recovery blob' (the BookmarkTree encrypted with the mnemonic-derived key) alongside the password-encrypted data. Uses @scure/bip39 (audited library, NOT custom mnemonic generation). Target: ≤150 lines.
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 3 (second third)

preserve_verbatim: Nothing — Holy PB has no recovery mechanism. Every competitor warns 'no password recovery.' This is entirely new.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB offers zero password recovery. If user forgets password, data is permanently lost.
falls_short.hush_requires: BIP39 mnemonic phrase that can recover encrypted data. User generates phrase on setup, writes it down, uses it to recover if password forgotten.

do_not_incorporate: Any custom mnemonic generation — must use @scure/bip39 audited library.
Storing mnemonic in browser storage (user must write it down physically).
build_from_scratch: deriveRecoveryPassword(phrase: string): Promise<string> — deterministic password from mnemonic (via mnemonicToSeed → hex string)
createRecoveryBlob(plaintext: string, phrase: string): Promise<EncryptedStore> — encrypt data with mnemonic-derived password
recoverFromBlob(blob: EncryptedStore, phrase: string): Promise<Result<string, RecoveryError | InvalidPasswordError>> — decrypt recovery blob
RECOVERY_KEY_PREFIX = 'hush_recovery_' constant — storage key prefix for recovery blobs
recoveryStorageKey(setId: string): string — computes recovery blob storage key
RecoveryError added to lib/errors.ts (or reuse ImportError pattern)
Repurpose RecoveryPhrase → RecoveryMetadata in lib/types.ts (remove words field, keep derivedKeyHash only)

user_stories.title: BIP39 mnemonic generation and validation
user_stories.description: Verify @scure/bip39 dependency and implement generateMnemonic and validateMnemonic. Must use the English wordlist from @scure/bip39/wordlists/english. The mnemonic is 12 words (128-bit entropy). Also repurpose the existing RecoveryPhrase type in lib/types.ts to RecoveryMetadata (remove words field, keep derivedKeyHash).
steps: PREREQUISITE: Verify @scure/bip39 is in package.json dependencies (not devDependencies). It is already installed — do NOT re-install.
RED: Write tests/unit/lib/recovery.test.ts. Test: 'generateMnemonic returns 12 words separated by spaces'
RED: Test: 'generateMnemonic returns valid BIP39 mnemonic' (validateMnemonic returns true)
RED: Test: 'generateMnemonic produces unique phrases' (generate 50, all different)
RED: Test: 'validateMnemonic returns true for valid mnemonic'
RED: Test: 'validateMnemonic returns false for random words'
RED: Test: 'validateMnemonic returns false for empty string'
RED: Test: 'validateMnemonic returns false for 11 words (too few)'
RED: Test: 'validateMnemonic returns false for wrong checksum (swap last word)'

GREEN: Create lib/recovery.ts. Import generateMnemonic as _generateMnemonic and validateMnemonic as _validateMnemonic from @scure/bip39. Import { wordlist } from '@scure/bip39/wordlists/english'. Wrap with typed exports.

GREEN: Repurpose RecoveryPhrase → RecoveryMetadata in lib/types.ts. Remove 'words: readonly string[]' field (mnemonic must never be stored). Keep 'derivedKeyHash: string' (proof that recovery was set up). Update the JSDoc to explain the type's purpose.
REFACTOR: Add JSDoc explaining BIP39 standard and 128-bit entropy
acceptance_criteria: generateMnemonic(): string — returns 12 words, space-separated, from English BIP39 wordlist
validateMnemonic(phrase: string): boolean — checksum verification via @scure/bip39
No custom mnemonic generation — delegates to @scure/bip39
RecoveryPhrase type renamed to RecoveryMetadata with words field removed
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/recovery.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Recovery key derivation and blob encryption/decryption
user_stories.description: Derive a deterministic password from the mnemonic phrase, then use it to encrypt/decrypt recovery blobs. The derived password is a hex string from the BIP39 seed. Recovery blobs use the same EncryptedStore format as regular encrypted data. Note: this intentionally runs PBKDF2 twice (once in mnemonicToSeed, once in encrypt) — acceptable because recovery is a rare operation, and reusing crypto.ts encrypt/decrypt keeps the module simple and the EncryptedStore format consistent.
steps: RED: Test: 'deriveRecoveryPassword returns consistent result for same phrase' (call twice, assert equal)
RED: Test: 'deriveRecoveryPassword returns different results for different phrases'
RED: Test: 'deriveRecoveryPassword returns non-empty string'
RED: Test: 'createRecoveryBlob produces valid EncryptedStore'
RED: Test: 'recoverFromBlob decrypts data encrypted by createRecoveryBlob' (roundtrip)
RED: Test: 'recoverFromBlob fails with wrong mnemonic phrase' (InvalidPasswordError)
RED: Test: 'recoverFromBlob fails with invalid blob data' (RecoveryError)
RED: Test: 'recovery blob is independent of user password' — encrypt with password A, create recovery with phrase B, recover with phrase B succeeds (data matches original)
RED: Test: 'recoveryStorageKey returns hush_recovery_{setId}' for set IDs
RED: Test: 'RECOVERY_KEY_PREFIX constant is hush_recovery_'

GREEN: Implement deriveRecoveryPassword (mnemonicToSeed → hex), createRecoveryBlob (encrypt with derived password), recoverFromBlob (decrypt with derived password). Import encrypt/decrypt from crypto.ts. Use async mnemonicToSeed (not sync — avoids blocking main thread in browser extension).
REFACTOR: Extract RecoveryError to errors.ts or decide to reuse existing error types
acceptance_criteria: deriveRecoveryPassword(phrase: string): Promise<string> — deterministic, uses async mnemonicToSeed from @scure/bip39
recoverFromBlob(blob: EncryptedStore, phrase: string): Promise<Result<string, RecoveryError | InvalidPasswordError>>
Recovery blob is EncryptedStore format (reuses crypto.ts encrypt/decrypt)
Wrong mnemonic returns InvalidPasswordError in Result
Invalid blob returns RecoveryError (or typed error) in Result
Recovery is independent of user password — different encryption paths
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/recovery.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Module purity, coverage, and integration verification
user_stories.description: Purity checks, scaffold-smoke update, full verification. Note: E2E skipped for this module — @scure/bip39 is pure JS with no browser API dependencies, so unit tests provide full coverage.
steps: Purity tests: zero React/DOM imports, zero console.log, zero type suppressions, zero empty catches
Verify @scure/bip39 is the ONLY external crypto dependency (no sjcl, no crypto-js)
scaffold-smoke.test.ts: add recovery.ts to LIB_MODULES (10 total — currently 9 modules + recovery.ts), exports callable test
Full verification: tsc + eslint + vitest + coverage + wxt build
Verify lib/recovery.ts ≤ 150 lines, coverage >= 80%
user_stories.verification: npx tsc --noEmit && npx eslint . && npx vitest run --coverage && npx wxt build

what_exists.modules_1_6: Modules 1-5 complete (implemented). Module 6 has PRD but not yet implemented. lib/crypto.ts (encrypt/decrypt), lib/storage.ts (263 lines, STORAGE_KEY), lib/data-model.ts (299 lines, generateId), lib/bookmark-import.ts (191 lines), lib/bookmark-backup.ts (~130 lines). LIB_MODULES = 9 in scaffold-smoke.
what_exists.errors: StorageError, InvalidPasswordError, DecryptionError, DataModelError, ImportError in lib/errors.ts.
what_exists.types: RecoveryPhrase type exists in lib/types.ts with 'words: readonly string[]' and 'derivedKeyHash: string'. RECOVERY-001 repurposes this to RecoveryMetadata (remove words, keep derivedKeyHash).
what_exists.test_infrastructure: happy-dom environment. scaffold-smoke tracks 9 LIB_MODULES. Build: ~594 kB.

what_does_not_exist.recovery: lib/recovery.ts does not exist. No mnemonic generation. No recovery mechanism.
what_does_not_exist.password_sets: lib/password-sets.ts does not exist yet (Module 6 not implemented). recoveryStorageKey(setId) takes a plain string — no hard dependency on Module 6.

tech_stack.external_dependency: @scure/bip39 — audited BIP39 implementation by @paulmillr. Lightweight (~12KB). API: generateMnemonic(wordlist, strength?), validateMnemonic(mnemonic, wordlist), mnemonicToSeed(mnemonic, passphrase?), mnemonicToSeedSync(mnemonic, passphrase?). Import wordlist from '@scure/bip39/wordlists/english'.
tech_stack.testing: Vitest with happy-dom. @scure/bip39 works in Node.js (no browser APIs needed for mnemonic generation).

recovery_flow.setup: 1. User creates password → 2. generateMnemonic() → 3. User writes down 12 words → 4. createRecoveryBlob(serializedTree, phrase) → 5. Store blob under recoveryStorageKey(setId)
recovery_flow.password_change: When password changes, recovery blob must be re-created with current data.
recovery_flow.recovery: 1. User enters 12 words → 2. validateMnemonic(phrase) → 3. recoverFromBlob(blob, phrase) → 4. User gets data back, must set new password → 5. New recovery blob created with new phrase or same phrase
recovery_flow.per_set: Each password set has its own recovery blob stored under 'hush_recovery_{setId}'. Recovery phrases can differ per set, or user can reuse same phrase (their choice, managed at UI level). Note: Module 6 (Password Sets) is a conceptual dependency — recoveryStorageKey(setId) takes a string, no code dependency.

design_decisions.double_pbkdf2: deriveRecoveryPassword converts mnemonic seed to hex string, then encrypt() runs PBKDF2 on that string. This is intentional: reusing crypto.ts encrypt/decrypt keeps the EncryptedStore format consistent and the module simple. Recovery is rare, so the performance cost is acceptable.
design_decisions.async_seed_derivation: Use async mnemonicToSeed (not mnemonicToSeedSync) to avoid blocking the main thread in a browser extension context.
design_decisions.no_mnemonic_storage: The mnemonic is NEVER stored in browser.storage. User writes it down physically. RecoveryMetadata stores only derivedKeyHash as proof recovery was set up.

inputs.what_it_provides: EncryptedStore, Result<T,E>, RecoveryPhrase (to be repurposed as RecoveryMetadata)

inputs.modification_needed: If adding RecoveryError: follows DataModelError context-object pattern.

outputs.consumed_by: Module 9 (Background — recovery flow orchestration), Module 10 (Popup — setup/recovery UI), Module 12 (Settings — view recovery phrase)
outputs.description: ≤150 lines. Exports: generateMnemonic, validateMnemonic, deriveRecoveryPassword, createRecoveryBlob, recoverFromBlob, recoveryStorageKey, RECOVERY_KEY_PREFIX.

known_risks.action: ~12KB. Tree-shakeable. Check wxt build size delta.

known_risks.issue: Double PBKDF2 in recovery path (mnemonicToSeed + encrypt)
known_risks.action: Intentional tradeoff — simplicity and format consistency over performance. Recovery is rare. Documented in design_decisions.

known_risks.issue: Recovery blob must be updated on every password change or data save
known_risks.action: Document this requirement for Module 9/10 (UI layer responsibility). lib/recovery.ts just provides the primitives.

known_risks.issue: User losing both password AND mnemonic = permanent data loss
known_risks.action: This is by design. Document clearly in UI. Cannot fix without weakening security model.

format_rules.format:
Append-only. Each entry: ## RECOVERY-XXX — <title>
- Status: PASS | FAIL (attempt N)
- Tests: N passing, N failing
- Coverage: N% branches
- Notes: <any issues encountered>
- Timestamp: ISO 8601
format_rules.update_trigger: After each story completion or failure

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session. Verify @scure/bip39 present. Generate recovery.ts + tests. Commit per story.
metadata.quality_gate: All tests pass. tsc clean. Only @scure/bip39 external dep. Coverage >= 80%. ≤ 150 lines.
metadata.human_review_required: Verify @scure/bip39 used (not custom). Verify mnemonic never stored in browser storage. Review deriveRecoveryPassword determinism.

execution_rules.clarifying_questions: Encouraged. Key: RecoveryError vs reusing ImportError.
